content:
# Exodus Chapter 7 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~34v–35r (PDF pp. 67–68); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 47–48 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_007_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_007_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_007_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_007_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "See, I have given you [as] a god to Par'oh, and Aharon your brother shall be your navi [prophet]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה רְאֵה נְתַתִּיךָ אֱלֹהִים לְפַרְעֹה וְאַהֲרֹן אָחִיךָ יִהְיֶה נְבִיאֶךָ (netatikha elohim le-Far'oh, "I have given you [as] a god to Par'oh"). The word elohim here functions as "god/divine representative" — Mosheh stands in God's place before Par'oh; Aharon is his navi (נָבִיא, "prophet/spokesman"). LXX (Vaticanus): ἰδοὺ δέδωκά σε θεὸν Φαραω, καὶ Ααρων ὁ ἀδελφός σου ἔσται σου προφήτης ("behold, I have given you [as] a god to Pharao, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet") — renders elohim as θεόν and navi as προφήτης, matching the Hebrew closely.

**2** You shall speak all that I command you, and Aharon your brother shall speak to Par'oh, and he shall send out the sons of Yisra'el from his land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אַתָּה תְדַבֵּר אֵת כָּל־אֲשֶׁר אֲצַוֶּךָּ וְאַהֲרֹן אָחִיךָ יְדַבֵּר אֶל־פַּרְעֹה וְשִׁלַּח אֶת־בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל מֵאַרְצוֹ (ve-shilakh, "and he shall send out"). LXX: σὺ δὲ λαλήσεις αὐτῷ πάντα ὅσα σοι ἐντέλλομαι ("and you shall speak to him all things which I command you") — adds αὐτῷ ("to him"), absent in MT.

**3** And I will strengthen [chazaq] the heart of Par'oh, and I will multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַאֲנִי אַקְשֶׁה אֶת־לֵב פַּרְעֹה — **CRITICAL CORRECTION**: LC actually reads וַאֲנִי אַקְשֶׁה (va'ani aqsheh), from the root קשה (qashah), "I will make hard," NOT חזק (chazaq). This is the hiphil imperfect of qashah. The verb here is **qashah** = "make hard." וְהִרְבֵּיתִי אֶת־אֹתֹתַי וְאֶת־מוֹפְתַי בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (ve-hirbeiti et-ototai ve-et-moftai, "and I will multiply my signs and my wonders"). LXX: ἐγὼ δὲ σκληρυνῶ τὴν καρδίαν Φαραω ("and I will harden the heart of Pharao") — σκληρύνω corresponds to qashah. **Hardening verb: qashah (קשה) = "make hard."**

**CORRECTED v.3:** And I will make hard [qashah] the heart of Par'oh, and I will multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Mitsrayim.

**4** And Par'oh will not listen to you, and I will give my hand against Mitsrayim, and I will bring out my hosts, my people the sons of Yisra'el, from the land of Mitsrayim by great judgments.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלֹא־יִשְׁמַע אֲלֵכֶם פַּרְעֹה וְנָתַתִּי אֶת־יָדִי בְּמִצְרָיִם וְהוֹצֵאתִי אֶת־צִבְאֹתַי אֶת־עַמִּי בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם בִּשְׁפָטִים גְּדֹלִים (tsiv'otai, "my hosts/armies"; bishfatim gedolim, "by great judgments"). LXX: καὶ ἐπιβαλῶ τὴν χεῖρά μου ἐπ᾽ Αἴγυπτον ("and I will lay my hand upon Egypt") — ἐπιβάλλω for the Hebrew נתן + יד ("give hand"). LXX adds: καὶ ἐξάξω τὴν δύναμίν μου ("and I will lead out my power/host").

**5** And the Mitsrim [Egyptians] shall know that I [am] YHWH, when I stretch out my hand upon Mitsrayim, and I bring out the sons of Yisra'el from their midst."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְיָדְעוּ מִצְרַיִם כִּי־אֲנִי יְהוָה בִּנְטֹתִי אֶת־יָדִי עַל־מִצְרָיִם וְהוֹצֵאתִי אֶת־בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל מִתּוֹכָם (bi-netoti et-yadi, "when I stretch out my hand"; mi-tokham, "from their midst"). LXX: καὶ γνώσονται πάντες οἱ Αἰγύπτιοι ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι κύριος ("and all the Egyptians shall know that I am Lord") — adds πάντες ("all"), not in MT.

**6** And Mosheh and Aharon did as YHWH commanded them; so they did.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַשׂ מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה אֹתָם כֵּן עָשׂוּ (ken asu, "so they did"). LXX: καθάπερ ἐνετείλατο αὐτοῖς κύριος, οὕτως ἐποίησαν ("just as the Lord commanded them, so they did").

**7** And Mosheh [was] a son of eighty years, and Aharon [was] a son of three and eighty years, when they spoke to Par'oh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמֹשֶׁה בֶּן־שְׁמֹנִים שָׁנָה וְאַהֲרֹן בֶּן־שָׁלֹשׁ וּשְׁמֹנִים שָׁנָה בְּדַבְּרָם אֶל־פַּרְעֹה (ben-shemonim shanah, "son of eighty years"; ben-shalosh u-shemonim, "son of three and eighty"). LXX: Μωυσῆς δὲ ἦν ἐτῶν ὀγδοήκοντα, Ααρων δὲ ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ ἐτῶν ὀγδοήκοντα τριῶν ("Moses was of eighty years, and Aaron his brother of eighty-three years").

**8** And YHWH spoke to Mosheh and to Aharon, saying:

**9** "When Par'oh speaks to you, saying, 'Give for yourselves a mofet [wonder/sign],' then you shall say to Aharon, 'Take your rod and throw [it] before Par'oh,' [and] it shall become a tannin [sea-serpent/dragon]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי יְדַבֵּר אֲלֵכֶם פַּרְעֹה לֵאמֹר תְּנוּ לָכֶם מוֹפֵת (tenu lakhem mofet, "give for yourselves a wonder"). וְאָמַרְתָּ אֶל־אַהֲרֹן קַח אֶת־מַטְּךָ וְהַשְׁלֵךְ לִפְנֵי־פַרְעֹה יְהִי לְתַנִּין (yehi le-tannin, "it shall become a tannin"). **Critical lexical note:** The word here is תַּנִּין (tannin), meaning "sea-serpent, sea-monster, dragon, great serpent" — this is NOT נָחָשׁ (nachash, "snake/serpent") as used in 4:3. The tannin is a larger, more mythologically charged creature, associated with cosmic chaos-monsters (cf. Gen 1:21; Isa 27:1; Eze 29:3 where Par'oh himself is called the tannin). LXX: δράκων (drakon, "dragon") — NOT ὄφις ("snake"), confirming the distinction.

**10** And Mosheh and Aharon went in to Par'oh, and they did so, as YHWH had commanded; and Aharon threw his rod before Par'oh and before his servants, and it became a tannin [sea-serpent/dragon].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּבֹא מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן אֶל־פַּרְעֹה וַיַּעַשׂוּ כֵן כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה וַיַּשְׁלֵךְ אַהֲרֹן אֶת־מַטֵּהוּ לִפְנֵי פַרְעֹה וְלִפְנֵי עֲבָדָיו וַיְהִי לְתַנִּין (vayehi le-tannin). LXX: καὶ ἔρριψεν Ααρων τὴν ῥάβδον... καὶ ἐγένετο δράκων ("and Aaron threw the rod... and it became a dragon").

**11** And Par'oh also called the khakhamim [wise men] and the mekhashfim [sorcerers]; and they also, the chartummim [magicians] of Mitsrayim, did so with their lahatim [secret arts/flames].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקְרָא גַּם־פַּרְעֹה לַחֲכָמִים וְלַמְכַשְּׁפִים וַיַּעֲשׂוּ גַם־הֵם חַרְטֻמֵּי מִצְרַיִם בְּלַהֲטֵיהֶם (khakhamim, "wise men"; mekhashfim, "sorcerers" from kashaf; chartummei Mitsrayim, "magicians of Egypt"; be-lahateihem, "with their secret arts"). The word חַרְטֻמִּים (chartummim) likely derives from Egyptian ḥry-tp ("chief lector-priest"); the word לַהֲטִים (lahatim) is related to לַהַט ("flame, blade") and connotes hidden/flaming arts. LXX: τοὺς σοφιστὰς... τοὺς φαρμακούς... οἱ ἐπαοιδοὶ τῶν Αἰγυπτίων... ταῖς φαρμακείαις αὐτῶν ("the sophists... the drug-makers... the enchanters of Egypt... with their sorceries").

**12** And they threw, each man, his rod, and they became tanninim [sea-serpents/dragons]; and the rod of Aharon swallowed their rods.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּשְׁלִיכוּ אִישׁ מַטֵּהוּ וַיִּהְיוּ לְתַנִּינִם וַיִּבְלַע מַטֵּה־אַהֲרֹן אֶת־מַטֹּתָם (vayivla matteh-Aharon et-mattotam, "and the rod of Aharon swallowed their rods"). Note: it is the rod (מַטֵּה) that swallows, not the tannin — the Hebrew specifically says "the rod of Aharon swallowed." LXX: καὶ κατέπιεν ἡ ῥάβδος ἡ Ααρων τὰς ἐκείνων ῥάβδους ("and the rod of Aaron swallowed up their rods") — agrees with MT that the rod, not the serpent, is the subject.

**13** And the heart of Par'oh was strengthened [chazaq], and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֶּחֱזַק לֵב פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא שָׁמַע אֲלֵהֶם כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה (vayekhezaq lev Par'oh, "and the heart of Par'oh was strengthened/was strong"). **Hardening verb: chazaq (חזק) = "strengthen."** This is the qal form — Par'oh's heart strengthened itself / was strong. It is not yet YHWH who is the agent. LXX: καὶ κατίσχυσεν ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("and the heart of Pharao prevailed/was strong") — κατισχύω corresponds to chazaq.

**14** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "The heart of Par'oh [is] heavy [kaved]; he has refused to send out the people.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה כָּבֵד לֵב פַּרְעֹה מֵאֵן לְשַׁלַּח הָעָם (kaved lev Par'oh, "heavy [is] the heart of Par'oh"; me'en le-shalakh, "he has refused to send out"). **Hardening verb: kaved (כבד) = "heavy."** This is an adjective: Par'oh's heart is heavy/dull/unresponsive. LXX: βεβάρηται ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("the heart of Pharao has been made heavy") — βαρέω corresponds precisely to kaved.

**15** Go to Par'oh in the morning — behold, he goes out to the water — and you shall stand to meet him upon the lip of the Ye'or [Nile], and the rod which was turned into a nachash [snake] you shall take in your hand.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֵךְ אֶל־פַּרְעֹה בַּבֹּקֶר הִנֵּה יֹצֵא הַמַּיְמָה (yotse ha-maymah, "going out to the water"). וְנִצַּבְתָּ לִקְרָאתוֹ עַל־שְׂפַת הַיְאֹר (al-sfat ha-Ye'or, "upon the lip of the Nile"). וְהַמַּטֶּה אֲשֶׁר־נֶהְפַּךְ לְנָחָשׁ תִּקַּח בְּיָדֶךָ (nehpakh le-nachash, "was turned to a nachash/snake"). Note: here the text says nachash (נָחָשׁ, "snake"), referring back to 4:3, not tannin as in 7:9-10 — this apparent discrepancy has occasioned much discussion. LXX: τὴν ῥάβδον τὴν στραφεῖσαν εἰς ὄφιν ("the rod that was turned into a snake") — uses ὄφις, not δράκων, matching the Hebrew switch from tannin to nachash.

**16** And you shall say to him: 'YHWH, the God of the Ivrim [Hebrews], has sent me to you, saying: Send out my people, and let them serve me in the wilderness; and behold, you have not listened until now.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאָמַרְתָּ אֵלָיו יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵי הָעִבְרִים שְׁלָחַנִי אֵלֶיךָ לֵאמֹר שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי וְיַעַבְדֻנִי בַּמִּדְבָּר וְהִנֵּה לֹא־שָׁמַעְתָּ עַד־כֹּה (ad-koh, "until thus/now"). LXX: κύριος ὁ θεὸς τῶν Εβραίων ἀπέσταλκέν με πρὸς σέ ("the Lord God of the Hebrews has sent me to you").

**17** Thus says YHWH: By this you shall know that I [am] YHWH — behold, I [am] striking with the rod which [is] in my hand upon the waters which [are] in the Ye'or [Nile], and they shall be turned to blood.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כֹּה אָמַר יְהוָה בְּזֹאת תֵּדַע כִּי אֲנִי יְהוָה הִנֵּה אָנֹכִי מַכֶּה בַּמַּטֶּה אֲשֶׁר־בְּיָדִי עַל־הַמַּיִם אֲשֶׁר בַּיְאֹר וְנֶהֶפְכוּ לְדָם (makeh, hiphil participle, "striking"; ve-nehefkhu le-dam, "and they shall be turned to blood"). **First plague: blood.** God speaks in first person — "I am striking" — though Aharon will perform the act (v.19-20). LXX: ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ τύπτω τῇ ῥάβδῳ τῇ ἐν τῇ χειρί μου ἐπὶ τὸ ὕδωρ τὸ ἐν τῷ ποταμῷ, καὶ μεταβαλεῖ εἰς αἷμα ("behold, I strike with the rod in my hand upon the water in the river, and it shall change to blood").

**18** And the fish which [are] in the Ye'or [Nile] shall die, and the Ye'or shall stink, and Mitsrayim shall be weary of drinking water from the Ye'or.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַדָּגָה אֲשֶׁר־בַּיְאֹר תָּמוּת וּבָאַשׁ הַיְאֹר וְנִלְאוּ מִצְרַיִם לִשְׁתּוֹת מַיִם מִן־הַיְאֹר (ha-dagah, "the fish"; tamut, "shall die"; u-va'ash, "and it shall stink"; ve-nil'u, niphal from la'ah, "they shall be weary/unable"). LXX: καὶ οἱ ἰχθύες οἱ ἐν τῷ ποταμῷ τελευτήσουσιν, καὶ ἐποζέσει ὁ ποταμός ("and the fish in the river shall die, and the river shall stink") — adds: καὶ οὐ δυνήσονται οἱ Αἰγύπτιοι πιεῖν ὕδωρ ἀπὸ τοῦ ποταμοῦ ("and the Egyptians shall not be able to drink water from the river").

**19** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Say to Aharon, 'Take your rod and stretch out your hand upon the waters of Mitsrayim — upon their rivers, upon their canals, and upon their pools, and upon every gathering of their waters — and they shall become blood; and blood shall be in all the land of Mitsrayim, and in the trees and in the stones.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה אֱמֹר אֶל־אַהֲרֹן קַח מַטְּךָ וּנְטֵה־יָדְךָ עַל־מֵימֵי מִצְרַיִם עַל־נַהֲרֹתָם עַל־יְאֹרֵיהֶם וְעַל־אַגְמֵיהֶם וְעַל כָּל־מִקְוֵה מֵימֵיהֶם וְיִהְיוּ־דָם (naharotam, "their rivers"; ye'oreihem, "their canals/Nile-branches"; agmeihem, "their pools/marshes"; kol-miqveh meimeihem, "every gathering of their waters"). וְהָיָה דָם בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם וּבָעֵצִים וּבָאֲבָנִים (u-va'etsim u-va'avanim, "and in the trees/wooden [vessels] and in the stones/stone [vessels]"). LXX: καὶ ἐπὶ πᾶν ξύλον αὐτῶν καὶ ἐπὶ πᾶν λίθον αὐτῶν — "and upon every wood of theirs and upon every stone of theirs," suggesting wooden and stone containers.

**20** And Mosheh and Aharon did so, as YHWH had commanded; and he lifted the rod and he struck the waters which [were] in the Ye'or [Nile] before the eyes of Par'oh and before the eyes of his servants, and all the waters which [were] in the Ye'or were turned to blood.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעֲשׂוּ כֵן מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה וַיָּרֶם אֶת־הַמַּטֶּה וַיַּךְ אֶת־הַמַּיִם אֲשֶׁר בַּיְאֹר לְעֵינֵי פַרְעֹה וּלְעֵינֵי עֲבָדָיו וַיֵּהָפְכוּ כָּל־הַמַּיִם אֲשֶׁר־בַּיְאֹר לְדָם (vayarem, "and he lifted"; vayakh, "and he struck"; vayehafkhu, "and they were turned"). The singular "he lifted/he struck" — likely referring to Aharon, though both are mentioned. LXX: καὶ ἐπάρας τῇ ῥάβδῳ αὐτοῦ ἐπάταξεν τὸ ὕδωρ τὸ ἐν τῷ ποταμῷ ("and lifting his rod he struck the water in the river") — clarifies with αὐτοῦ ("his").

**21** And the fish which [was] in the Ye'or [Nile] died, and the Ye'or stank, and Mitsrayim were not able to drink water from the Ye'or; and the blood was in all the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַדָּגָה אֲשֶׁר־בַּיְאֹר מֵתָה וַיִּבְאַשׁ הַיְאֹר וְלֹא־יָכְלוּ מִצְרַיִם לִשְׁתּוֹת מַיִם מִן־הַיְאֹר וַיְהִי הַדָּם בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (metah, "died"; vayiv'ash, "and it stank"; lo-yakhlu, "they were not able"). LXX: καὶ ἐτελεύτησαν οἱ ἰχθύες... καὶ ἐπώζεσεν ὁ ποταμός ("and the fish died... and the river stank").

**22** And the chartummim [magicians] of Mitsrayim did so with their lahatim [secret arts]; and the heart of Par'oh was strengthened [chazaq], and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעֲשׂוּ־כֵן חַרְטֻמֵּי מִצְרַיִם בְּלָטֵיהֶם (be-lateihem — note: here the word is בְּלָטֵיהֶם, from לָט "secrecy/covering," slightly different spelling from בְּלַהֲטֵיהֶם in v.11 though same meaning: "with their secret arts"). וַיֶּחֱזַק לֵב־פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא־שָׁמַע אֲלֵהֶם כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה (vayekhezaq lev-Par'oh, "and the heart of Par'oh was strengthened"). **Hardening verb: chazaq (חזק) = "strengthen."** Again the qal — Par'oh's heart strengthened itself. LXX: καὶ ἐσκληρύνθη ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("and the heart of Pharao was hardened") — interestingly uses σκληρύνω (normally for qashah) rather than κατισχύω (used for chazaq in v.13).

**23** And Par'oh turned and went into his house, and he did not set his heart also to this.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּפֶן פַּרְעֹה וַיָּבֹא אֶל־בֵּיתוֹ וְלֹא־שָׁת לִבּוֹ גַּם־לָזֹאת (vayifen, "and he turned"; ve-lo-shat libbo gam-la-zo't, "and he did not set his heart also to this"). The verb שָׁת (shat) is from שִׁית (shit), "to set/place." Par'oh does not even take the sign to heart. LXX: ἐπιστραφεὶς δὲ Φαραω εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ, καὶ οὐκ ἐπέστησεν τὸν νοῦν αὐτοῦ οὐδὲ ἐπὶ τούτῳ ("and Pharao, having turned, entered his house, and did not set his mind even upon this").

**24** And all Mitsrayim dug around the Ye'or [Nile] [for] water to drink, for they were not able to drink from the waters of the Ye'or.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּחְפְּרוּ כָל־מִצְרַיִם סְבִיבֹת הַיְאֹר מַיִם לִשְׁתּוֹת כִּי לֹא יָכְלוּ לִשְׁתֹּת מִמֵּימֵי הַיְאֹר (vayakhperu, "and they dug"; sevivot, "around/surroundings"; mi-meimei, "from the waters of"). LXX: καὶ ὤρυξαν πάντες οἱ Αἰγύπτιοι κύκλῳ τοῦ ποταμοῦ ("and all the Egyptians dug round about the river").

**25** And seven days were filled after YHWH struck the Ye'or [Nile].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּמָּלֵא שִׁבְעַת יָמִים אַחֲרֵי הַכּוֹת־יְהוָה אֶת־הַיְאֹר (vayimmale shiv'at yamim, "and seven days were filled/completed"; akharei hakot-YHWH, "after the striking of YHWH" — hakot is the hiphil infinitive construct of nakah, "to strike"). LXX: καὶ ἀνεπληρώθησαν ἑπτὰ ἡμέραι μετὰ τὸ πατάξαι κύριον τὸν ποταμόν ("and seven days were fulfilled after the Lord struck the river").

---

## Hardening-Verb Tracking — Exodus 7

| Verse | Hebrew Root | Verb Form | Translation | Agent |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 7:3 | קשה (qashah) | Hiphil impf. אַקְשֶׁה | "I will make hard" | YHWH (predictive) |
| 7:13 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 7:14 | כבד (kaved) | Adjective כָּבֵד | "[is] heavy" | Par'oh's heart (state) |
| 7:22 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |

## Key Lexical Notes — Exodus 7

- **tannin** (תַּנִּין, vv. 9, 10, 12): "sea-serpent/dragon" — distinct from nachash (נָחָשׁ, "snake") in 4:3 and 7:15. The LXX confirms this with δράκων vs. ὄφις.
- **chartummim** (חַרְטֻמִּים, vv. 11, 22): "magicians" — Egyptian lector-priests; probably from Egyptian ḥry-tp.
- **lahatim / latim** (לַהֲטִים / לָטִים, vv. 11, 22): "secret arts" — v.11 uses the fuller form בְּלַהֲטֵיהֶם; v.22 uses the shorter בְּלָטֵיהֶם.
- **mofet** (מוֹפֵת, v. 9): "wonder/sign" — distinct from 'ot (אוֹת, "sign"); mofet emphasizes the portentous, wonder-producing quality.
- **Ye'or** (יְאֹר): The Nile — from Egyptian itrw/ỉꜣrw. Used 7 times in this chapter (vv. 15, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 24, 25).
